{{Quote|I held a single-entrance fortress similar to this on Amador. Enemy bodies were stacked twenty-high around the outside when that battle was over.|Fideltin Rusk|<ref name="Ambient" />}}
'''Amador''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Chalenor system]]<ref name="Acts" /> of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region's]] [[Immalia sector/Legends|Immalia sector]],<ref name="Atlas" /> and was orbited by at least one [[Moon/Legends|moon]] named [[Mayvitch 7]].<ref name="Acts" /> Situated in the [[Northern Dependencies/Legends|Northern Dependencies]],<ref name="Region" /> Amador lay at the junction of a [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] that connected the [[Coth Fuuras system/Legends|Coth Fuuras system]] and the planet [[Nessem]] and a [[Amador—Drearia hyperlane|second route]] that linked it to the [[Drearia system/Legends|Drearia system]].<ref name="Warfare" /> Sometime between [[3665 BBY|3665]]<ref name="date">The ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic Encyclopedia]]'' indicates [[Elin Garza]] forged her identity credentials at the age of 15 to enlist in the [[Republic Military/Legends|Republic Military]], meaning that the minimum age must have been greater than 15. The same guide also gives [[Fideltin Rusk]]'s birth [[Standard year/Legends|year]] as [[3680 BBY]], meaning that Rusk had to enlist sometime after [[3665 BBY]]. Therefore, the [[Battle on Amador|fighting]] on Amador must have occurred after 3665 BBY.</ref> and [[3653 BBY]],<ref name="Timeline 1">{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant|int=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant}}</ref> a [[Battle on Amador|battle]] was fought on Amador during the [[Great Galactic War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|Sith Empire]], where the [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Fideltin Rusk]] defended the entrance of a [[Amador fortress|fortress]] on Amador from a much larger force of [[Imperial soldier]]s.<ref name="Ambient" /> Over three thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, a group of [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonists on Mayvitch 7 were [[Battle of Mayvitch 7|wiped out]] by the [[Yinchorri/Legends|Yinchorri]] [[Species/Legends|species]] during the [[Yinchorri Uprising]] of [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]].<ref name="Acts" />

==Behind the scenes==
Amador was first mentioned in the [[Jedi Council: Acts of War 1|first issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Jedi Council: Acts of War|''Jedi Council: Acts of War'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series. The issue was written by [[Randy Stradley]] and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="Acts" /> on [[June 21]], [[2000]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54290/star_wars_jedi_council_-_acts_of_war_2000_1|text=Star Wars: Jedi Council - Acts of War (2000) #1}}</ref> The planet was not mentioned again until the [[2011]] [[Star Wars video games|massively-multiplayer online roleplaying video game]] ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', where the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] class's companion character Fideltin Rusk mentions Amador during ambient conversation that is triggered when he arrives at the [[Shrine of Healing]] on the planet [[Voss]].<ref name="Ambient" />
